questions.prompt: This camera ______ very light, so I carry it everywhere.
We ______ photos of the old town every weekend.
My cousin works in ______ office in the city centre.
My phone ______ have enough space for long videos.
The group always meets ______ half past six.
Last Sunday we ______ to the harbour to photograph the sunrise.
The new lens is ______ than my old one, so my bag feels full.
How ______ pictures did you take during the trip?
The streets are quiet at dawn, ______ we always start early.
Tomorrow we ______ travel to the coast by train.
I ______ this camera three years ago, and it still works perfectly.
If the sky ______ clear tonight, we will photograph the stars.
If I had a better lens, I ______ enter the competition.
The exhibition ______ by a group of students last spring.
I ______ take photos on film, but now I use a digital camera.
She ______ on the same picture since eight o'clock this morning.
If we ______ the timetable, we would not have missed the last ferry.
The organiser said that the results ______ announced the following week.
This is the rooftop ______ we took the night shots of the harbour.
I wish I ______ brought a spare battery with me yesterday.
The pictures ______ have been deleted by mistake — they are not on the card.
Rarely ______ such clear light over the city at this time of year.
Had we set off earlier, we ______ at the viewpoint by now.
It was not until midnight ______ the lights on the bridge finally came on.
You ______ have carried all that equipment — I could easily have brought the car.
Choose the word from the bank that has the same meaning as each word below.
Match each photography word with its definition.

bank: a three-legged stand that holds a camera steady
the piece of glass that focuses light inside a camera
a picture of a person, usually showing the face
a picture of wide natural scenery
a public display of pictures for people to look at
a dark room in which photographs used to be developed
a short description printed underneath a picture
a padded bag for carrying equipment safely
a device that stores electrical power
a person who takes pictures for a newspaper

answers: a three-legged stand that holds a camera steady
the piece of glass that focuses light inside a camera
a picture of a person, usually showing the face
a picture of wide natural scenery
a public display of pictures for people to look at

questions.prompt: Choose the word from the bank that best completes each sentence.

items.text: Please ______ your three best photos to the club website before Friday.
If the light is too bright, ______ the settings on your camera.
A skilled photographer can ______ a moment that lasts less than a second.
You should ______ a place on the weekend course early, as it fills up quickly.
Experts managed to ______ the damaged photograph to its original condition.

questions.prompt: Choose the word from the bank that goes with each verb to make a common expression.
Match each travel word with the phrase that explains it.

bank: a detailed plan of the stages of a journey
a well-known building or feature that helps you recognise a place
an object kept to remind you of a place you have visited
a longer route taken to avoid a problem on the direct road
a spare copy of files kept in case the original is lost
an official document allowing you to enter a country
the money charged for a journey on public transport
a person whose job is to show visitors around a place
a short rest taken in the middle of a long journey
a device that shows your exact position on a map

answers: a detailed plan of the stages of a journey
a well-known building or feature that helps you recognise a place
an object kept to remind you of a place you have visited
a longer route taken to avoid a problem on the direct road
a spare copy of files kept in case the original is lost
